nonGoals: Do not modify scorer.go, pipeline.go, or any report files
Do not add the Unsupported field to CertificationRecord
objective: Create the language_tiers package with tier constants, TierForLanguage function, and parser-capability checking
planText: Create internal/language_tiers/language_tiers.go: define a Tier type (TierGeneric, TierFull), implement TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier using analysis.ForLanguage() to check parser availability — languages with a registered analyzer (go, ts, py, rs) are TierFull, everything else is TierGeneric. Create internal/language_tiers/language_tiers_test.go with table-driven tests covering each supported language returning TierFull and unsupported languages returning TierGeneric.

nonGoals: Do not modify scorer.go, pipeline.go, or any report files
Do not alter any existing grade constants
objective: Add GradeNA constant and Unsupported field to domain types to support the N/A short-circuit path
planText: In internal/domain/dimension.go, add GradeNA Grade = "N/A" constant alongside existing grade constants. In internal/domain/record.go, add Unsupported bool field to CertificationRecord struct. Both changes are purely additive — zero-value bool defaults to false preserving existing behaviour.

nonGoals: Do not modify report/detailed.go, report/full.go, report/health.go, or report/card.go
Do not add aggregate-skip logic outside the engine package
Do not create the language_tiers package or domain types (those are prior deliverables)
objective: Guard Score() with language-tier gate, protect complexity maintainability rule, remove 0.5 defaults, update pipeline, and fix all engine tests
planText: Modify internal/engine/scorer.go: (a) Import language_tiers and domain packages. (b) Change Score() signature to accept a language string parameter. (c) At the top of Score(), call language_tiers.TierForLanguage(lang); if TierGeneric, return domain.DimensionScores(nil) — caller will handle the N/A short-circuit. (d) In scoreFromMetrics, guard the complexity ≤ 5 → 0.95 maintainability rule: skip when complexity == 0 AND the evidence's language has no parser (TierGeneric). Pass language into scoreFromMetrics. (e) Remove the violation else-branch that injects dimensions at 0.80 minus penalty (lines 65-74); only the if-branch that applies penalties to existing dimensions remains. (f) Update the Score() doc comment to state that dimensions without evidence are absent and TierGeneric units receive nil scores. Modify internal/engine/pipeline.go: (a) Pass unit.ID.Language() to Score(). (b) After Score() returns, if scores == nil, set record.Grade = domain.GradeNA, record.Unsupported = true, and return immediately without computing avg/status. Update internal/engine/scorer_test.go: update all Score() calls to pass a language string; add test cases for TierGeneric short-circuit (swift → nil scores), zero-complexity guard (generic file with complexity=0 must NOT get maintainability=0.95), and violation handling without 0.5 injection (violation on absent dimension → no dimension entry). Update internal/engine/pipeline_test.go if needed.

nonGoals: Do not modify scorer.go, pipeline.go, or domain types
Do not change the N/A rendering logic (already handled by existing gradeEmoji fallback)
Do not add new report output formats
objective: Update report consumers to skip Unsupported records in aggregate calculations
planText: In internal/report/detailed.go computeDimensionAverages: skip records where Unsupported == true when summing and counting. In internal/report/full.go buildLanguageDetail: skip Unsupported records when computing per-language averages. In internal/report/health.go Health(): skip Unsupported records in pass/fail counts and score averaging. In internal/report/card.go: skip Unsupported records from the total count used in GenerateCard(). Existing tests in card_test.go already handle N/A for empty records and should continue to pass.
